title: Tender Canadian Beetroot Roast
description: A hearty Canadian dish featuring roasted beetroot, perfect for a family dinner, with anti-inflammatory properties from the beetroot and a crumbly texture from the fatty roast.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
Place the cubed beetroot, chopped onion, and minced garlic in a bowl and toss with olive oil, salt, black pepper, and thyme until they are evenly coated.
Spread the beetroot mixture in a single layer in a large roasting pan.
Season the brisket with salt and black pepper, then place it on top of the beetroot mixture in the roasting pan.
Roast the brisket and beetroot mixture in the preheated oven for 3 hours, or until the brisket is tender and the beetroot is caramelized.
Add the chopped carrots to the roasting pan and continue to roast for an additional 30 minutes, or until the carrots are tender.
Remove the roasting pan from the oven and let it rest for 15 minutes before slicing the brisket and serving with the roasted beetroot and carrots.
Slice the brisket against the grain and serve with the roasted beetroot and carrots.
